name: White Dragon Horse
description: The White Dragon Horse, originally a dragon prince named Ao Lie, is a pivotal figure in the classic Chinese novel *Journey to the West*. He was the youngest son of the Dragon King of the West Sea. Due to an act of arson – accidentally setting fire to the Dragon King's pearl – he was sentenced to death. However, Guanyin, the Bodhisattva of Compassion, intervened and spared his life, transforming him into a horse and ordering him to await the arrival of the Tang Monk, Xuanzang. He became Xuanzang's loyal steed on the perilous journey to retrieve the sacred scriptures from the Leiyin Temple in the West. Throughout the journey, the White Dragon Horse faithfully carried Xuanzang across vast distances and through treacherous landscapes, facing numerous demons and obstacles. He often transformed back into his dragon form to assist the pilgrimage, showcasing his strength and courage. After the successful completion of the journey, the White Dragon Horse was rewarded for his loyalty and dedication, being restored to his original dragon form and granted a high rank in the heavens. He is revered as a symbol of repentance, redemption, loyalty, and unwavering commitment to a noble cause.
subtitle: Loyal Steed of the Tang Monk
achievements: Carried Xuanzang across the Journey to the West
Transformed into a dragon to fight demons
Redeemed himself from his past transgression
Achieved enlightenment and a high rank in Heaven

enemies.name: Various demons encountered on the journey

keyMyths.title: Transformation and Redemption
keyMyths.description: Ao Lie, the dragon prince, was transformed into a horse by Guanyin as punishment and a chance for redemption. He was tasked with serving Xuanzang on his journey to the West, allowing him to atone for his past actions.

keyMyths.title: Loyalty and Service
keyMyths.description: The White Dragon Horse faithfully carried Xuanzang throughout the arduous journey, facing countless dangers and challenges. His unwavering loyalty and dedication were crucial to the success of the pilgrimage.

keyMyths.title: Return to Dragon Form
keyMyths.description: Upon completing the journey and retrieving the sacred scriptures, the White Dragon Horse was restored to his original dragon form. He was also granted a high rank in Heaven as a reward for his service and devotion.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
sources.description: Classic Chinese novel detailing the pilgrimage of Xuanzang and his disciples.
sources.text: Classic Chinese novel detailing the pilgrimage of Xuanzang and his disciples.

sources.author: Yu, Anthony C.
sources.description: English translation of the classic novel by Wu Cheng'en.
sources.text: English translation of the classic novel by Wu Cheng'en.
